expectations.text: internalEdges contains svc→core (workspace:* protocol) and web→core (file:../core link) — both recognized despite the different protocols
the svc→test-kit devDependencies edge is present and flagged as dev-only (it must not be presented as an ordinary runtime edge)
edges use layer names from the provided list (core, svc, web, test-kit), not the scoped package names (@edge/core etc.)
the legacy layer (no package.json) appears in gaps as having no recognizable manifest — not silently skipped, and no edges invented for it
expectations.evidence: legacy gaps=[{"layer":"legacy","issue":"No package.json at packages/legacy/ (only src/index.js) — dependencies unknowable from manifests; layer cannot be placed in the dependency graph and must not be silently skipped."}], legacy edges=[]

expectations.text: externalDeps name the defining deps: fastify for svc, react for web, zod for core, vitest for test-kit
ordering identifies core as the provider consumed by svc and web (changes first); test-kit's provider role, if mentioned, is qualified as dev-only
expectations.evidence: ordering={"providers":["core","test-kit"],"leaves":["svc","web"],"notes":["core is the central provider, consumed by both svc (workspace:*) and web (file:../core) — protocol differs but both are internal edges; core changes first when tasks span layers.","test-kit is a provider consumed only as a devDependency of svc (test utils); dev-only mark is informational — deriveDispatchOrder weighs it like any other edge, so test-kit still orders before svc.","svc and web are leaf consumers no other layer depends on; they change last.","No layer consumes legacy and legacy declares nothing (no manifest) — an island, but its true position is unknowable until its manifest gap is resolved."],"suggestedOrder":["core","test-kit","svc","web"]}
